'''Xerxes''' was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TRG1" /> located in the [[Xerxes system/Legends|Xerxes system]] of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA" /> During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], it had a [[Xerxes planetary police|planetary police]] force and was the site of a [[Unidentified weapons shop|weapons shop]].<ref name="TRG1" />

At one point during that conflict, the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Roark Garnet/Legends|Roark Garnet]] visited Xerxes, where the local [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police]] were looking for him. He wished to visit a [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] shop but, wary of an ambush, spent around an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] watching the establishment for any signs of a stake-out. Ultimately, while still not convinced it was safe to enter, Garnet attempted to recruit the assistance of passersby before offering a [[Unidentified Human (Xerxes)|Human pedestrian]] twenty [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to go into the shop, look around it, and then report to the smuggler of what he saw inside.<ref name="TRG1" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Harron-VoyagesSF13.png|thumb|right|250px|Xerxes placed on a map of the Harron sector included in ''Voyages SF'' 13.]]
Xerxes was first mentioned in the [[1987]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|rulebook]] for the first edition of [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="TRG1" /> It shares its name with "Emperor Ford Xerxes XII" and "Alexander Xerxes XII," character names that were written down in [[1973]] by the ''[[Star Wars]]'' franchise's creator, [[George Lucas]], during the early writing stages of<ref name="Making">''[[The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film]]''</ref> the first part of the [[original trilogy]], the [[1977]] film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], and that eventually morphed into the character of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="YearByYear">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref>

"[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, placed Xerxes in the [[Harron sector/Legends|Harron sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Xerxes [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square M-11, also overriding the ''Voyages SF'' 13 placement by establishing that it was situated outside of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]]<ref name="TEA" /> in which the Harron [[Sector/Legends|sector]] was later placed.<ref name="Expansion">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-expansion-region|text=Essential Atlas Extra: Expansion Region}}</ref>
